input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compiler toolchain |
| **.NET SDK** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android build tooling |

---

## 2. Installation Steps

```bash
# 1. Install Xcode from App Store, then accept license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 2. Install .NET SDK (latest LTS — currently .NET 8):
#    Download the macOS installer from https://dot.net/download

# 3. Install the MAUI workload (handles Android SDK, JDK, emulator):
dotnet workload install maui

# 4. (Optional) Install Android command-line tools manually if needed:
#    https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools
```

The `maui` workload pulls in: `android`, `ios`, `maccatalyst`, and required Android SDK components.

---

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See installed SDKs:
dotnet --list-sdks

# See available/installed workloads:
dotnet workload list

# Check which MAUI version maps to your SDK:
dotnet workload search maui

# Check Xcode version requirements:
#   https://github.com/xamarin/xamarin-macios/wiki/Minimum-Xcode-version
```

Microsoft publishes a support matrix at:  
**[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)**

---

## 4. Verify the Setup

```bash
# Confirm workloads:
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, android, ios, maccatalyst

# Create and build a test app:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode):
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android:
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first):
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

---

## 5. IDE (Optional but Recommended)

- **VS Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + .NET MAUI extension
- **Visual Studio for Mac** was retired — VS Code is now the official path on macOS
- **JetBrains Rider** also supports MAUI

---

## Key Tips

- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- For physical iOS devices, configure an Apple Developer account in Xcode → Settings → Accounts.
- Android emulator on Apple Silicon uses the ARM64 system image — no HAXM needed.
